task_completion.description: Verify JStock is open with the three initial watchlist tabs visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is visible, displaying tabs for 'My Watchlist', 'Tech Stocks', and 'Energy Stocks'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent initiates the deletion of the 'Energy Stocks' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent opens a context menu (e.g., right-clicking the tab) or a top menu dropdown (e.g., Watchlist -> Delete) with the 'Delete' option highlighted while 'Energy Stocks' is the target.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent confirms the deletion action.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation dialog asking to delete the 'Energy Stocks' watchlist is visible, and the agent clicks 'Yes', 'OK', or the equivalent confirmation button.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Energy Stocks' watchlist has been successfully deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Energy Stocks' tab is no longer visible anywhere in the JStock application window.

task_completion.description: Verify the other watchlists were preserved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'My Watchlist' and 'Tech Stocks' tabs remain clearly visible and open in the JStock application window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the JStock graphical user interface to delete the watchlist.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show mouse clicks and menu navigations within the JStock app, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use the terminal to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing 'rm' commands targeting the JStock watchlist directory.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction occurred in the actual JStock application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JStock application window with its standard stock tracking interface is visible, not a text editor or file manager modifying JStock configuration files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target watchlist to delete is exactly 'Energy Stocks'. The watchlists 'My Watchlist' and 'Tech Stocks' must be preserved. The expected final count of watchlists in the JStock interface is exactly 2.
